name: Flourless Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Butter the sides of an 8 x 2-inch cake pan and line the base with a circle of baking parchment, then butter the parchment.
3. Using a nut mill, grind almonds to flour and set aside.
4. Grate carrots into fine shreds using the same nut mill. There should be about 1 1/2 cups of shredded carrots, packed firmly in a bowl.
5. Add lemon zest, lemon juice, and set aside.
6. Beat egg whites until they start to stiffen. Gradually sprinkle in half the sugar while continuing to beat until stiff and glossy.
7. Beat egg yolks with the remaining sugar until very thick and pale yellow in color. Stir in the carrot mixture and ground almonds. Fold in the beaten egg whites.
8. Sift cornstarch and baking powder over the batter and fold in lightly but thoroughly.
9. Spoon the batter into the prepared pan, smooth the top with a rubber spatula, and sprinkle with pine nuts.
10. Bake for 40 minutes, or until a tester comes out clean. Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes.
11. Run a knife around the edge of the cake and carefully unmold onto a rack. Turn the parchment side up and let cool completely.
12. Dust the edges with a 2-inch wide band of powdered sugar before serving.
Serves 8.
Per Serving: 245 calories, 7 g protein, 30 g carbohydrate, 12 g fat (2 g saturated), 79 mg cholesterol, 56 mg sodium, 3 g fiber.
